+        "title": "Check current lima setup",

+        "title": "Review lima configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant lima configuration:\nI just learned that on macOS Ventura you can register Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler inside a VM running in arm64 mode."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura",
+        "description": "> This needs implementing Virtualization.framework support (#889) right?\n\nI don't know; it depends on if we can get VirtioFS support without it.\n\nOr if it will be possible to somehow bridge between the VirtioFS volume and qemu in some other way. I suspect not, as event notification will be a challenge, but it doesn't hurt to try...\n\nI suspect that the binfmt_misc handler is just copying stuff to the Rosetta volume; Rosetta will perform the translation on the host and put the results back into\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> This needs implementing Virtualization.framework support (#889) right?\n\nI don't know; it depends on if we can get VirtioFS support without it.\n\nOr if it will be possible to somehow bridge between the VirtioFS volume and qemu in some other way. I suspect not, as event notification will be a challenge, but it doesn't hurt to try...",
